test cases.title: Successful User Registration with Valid Credentials
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
Email address is not already registered.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter a unique, valid email address.
Enter a password meeting complexity requirements (e.g., min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number, 1 special char).
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully registered and redirected to a confirmation screen or logged in automatically. A verification email should be sent (if applicable).

test cases.title: Registration with Existing Email Address
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
An account already exists with the entered email address.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter an already registered email address.
test cases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Email already registered' or similar should be displayed.

test cases.title: Registration with Invalid Email Format
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter an email address in an invalid format (e.g., 'test@.com', 'test@com', 'test.com').
test cases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Invalid email format' should be displayed, and registration should not proceed.

test cases.title: Registration with Password Not Meeting Complexity Requirements
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter a valid, unique email address.
Enter a password that does not meet complexity rules (e.g., 'short', 'onlylowercase').
test cases.expectedResult: An inline error message indicating password complexity requirements (e.g., 'Password must be at least 8 characters long and contain uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character') should be displayed, and registration should not proceed.

test cases.title: Registration with Mismatched Passwords
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter a valid, unique email address.
Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field.
Enter a different password in the 'Confirm Password' field.
test cases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Passwords do not match' should be displayed, and registration should not proceed.

test cases.title: Network Disconnection During Registration Submission
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen with valid credentials entered.
steps: Turn off network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device.
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate network error message should be displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection', 'Failed to connect'). Upon network restoration, user should be able to retry or informed on state.

test cases.title: Successful Login with Linked Google Account
preconditions: User has an existing account linked to a Google account.
User is logged into their Google account on the mobile device.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Select the linked Google account from the system prompt (if multiple accounts present).
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: User Cancels Google Login Prompt
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
Network connection is stable.
steps: When the Google authentication prompt appears, tap 'Cancel' or equivalent.
test cases.expectedResult: The Google authentication prompt is dismissed, and the user remains on the Login screen without logging in.

test cases.title: Attempt Login with Google Account Not Linked to an Existing User
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
User has a Google account that is not linked to any existing application account.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Select the unlinked Google account from the system prompt.
test cases.expectedResult: The system should prompt the user to register or link the Google account to a new application account. An appropriate message should be displayed.

test cases.title: Network Disconnection During Google SSO Flow
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
steps: Turn off network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device during the Google authentication process.
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate network error message should be displayed. The Google SSO flow should gracefully handle the interruption and allow the user to retry upon network restoration.

test cases.title: Login with Google after Revoking App Permissions
preconditions: User has previously logged in with Google and then revoked app permissions from their Google account settings.
User is on the Login screen.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Proceed through the Google authentication prompt.
test cases.expectedResult: The Google prompt should require the user to re-grant permissions. Upon granting, login should be successful. If permissions are denied, user remains on login screen with an error.

test cases.title: Successful Forgot Password Request for Registered Email
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
An account exists for the email address.
Network connection is stable.
test cases.expectedResult: A confirmation message is displayed (e.g., 'Password reset email sent. Check your inbox.'). A password reset email should be received in the inbox for the entered email address.

test cases.title: Forgot Password Request for Unregistered Email
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
The email address is not registered in the system.
Network connection is stable.
test cases.expectedResult: A discreet message implying success (to prevent enumeration attacks, e.g., 'If an account exists, a reset link has been sent.') should be displayed. No email should be sent.

test cases.title: Successfully Reset Password via Valid Link
preconditions: User has received a valid password reset email.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Open the password reset email on a device.
On the password reset page, enter a new password meeting complexity requirements.
Attempt to log in with the new password.
test cases.expectedResult: Password is successfully updated. User is able to log in with the new password. The reset link should become invalid after use.

test cases.title: Attempt to Use Expired or Invalid Password Reset Link
preconditions: User has a password reset link that has expired or is malformed/invalid.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Open the expired/invalid password reset link.
test cases.expectedResult: An error page or message should be displayed indicating the link is invalid or expired, and prompt the user to request a new one.

test cases.title: Request Multiple Password Resets in Quick Succession
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password screen.
An account exists for the email address.
Network connection is stable.
steps: Enter a registered email address and tap 'Send Reset Link'.
Immediately repeat step 1 several times within a short period.
test cases.expectedResult: The system should handle this gracefully, either by rate-limiting subsequent requests, sending only the latest link, or informing the user of a cooldown period. Previous links should be invalidated by newer ones.

test cases.title: Network Disconnection During Forgot Password Request Submission
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password screen with a valid email entered.
steps: Turn off network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device.
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate network error message should be displayed. Upon network restoration, user should be able to retry or informed on state.

test cases.title: Submission with Empty Email Field
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen.
test cases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Email is required' should be displayed, and submission should be prevented.

test cases.title: Submission with Empty Password Field (Registration)
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Leave the password and confirm password fields empty.
test cases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Password is required' should be displayed, and submission should be prevented.

test cases.title: Email Input with Leading/Trailing Spaces
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen.
steps: Enter an email address with leading or trailing spaces (e.g., '  test@example.com  ').
test cases.expectedResult: The system should trim leading/trailing spaces from the email address and proceed with valid input, or display an error if the trimmed input is invalid.

test cases.title: Password Input with Leading/Trailing Spaces
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a password with leading or trailing spaces (e.g., '  password123  ').
test cases.expectedResult: The system should treat leading/trailing spaces as part of the password for complexity and matching, or display an error if spaces are not allowed.

test cases.title: Input Exceeding Expected Maximum Length for Email
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen.
steps: Enter an extremely long but otherwise valid email address (e.g., 256+ characters).
test cases.expectedResult: The input field should either prevent further typing beyond a defined max length, or display an error message 'Email address is too long' upon submission.

test cases.title: Input Exceeding Expected Maximum Length for Password
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter an extremely long password (e.g., 100+ characters).
test cases.expectedResult: The input field should either prevent further typing beyond a defined max length, or display an error message 'Password is too long' upon submission.

test cases.title: Verify Average Login Time (Email/Password)
preconditions: User has a registered account.
Network connection is stable and typical for a mobile user.
Performance monitoring tools are active.
steps: Tap 'Login' button and measure the time until the dashboard/home screen is fully loaded.
test cases.expectedResult: The average login time should be consistently under 3 seconds across multiple attempts.

test cases.title: App Sent to Background During Registration Flow
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen with partially filled form.
steps: Start filling out the registration form.
Send the app to the background (e.g., by navigating to home screen or another app).
Bring the app back to the foreground after a short period (e.g., 10-30 seconds).
test cases.expectedResult: The app should resume the registration flow with the entered data preserved (if within a reasonable time limit), or gracefully reset to a default state if data persistence is not intended.

test cases.title: Network State Change During Login Submission
preconditions: User is on the Login screen with valid credentials entered.
steps: Toggle network connection (e.g., switch from Wi-Fi to mobile data, or temporarily disable/enable) right before or during tapping 'Login'.
test cases.expectedResult: The application should handle the network transition gracefully, either retrying the request, displaying an appropriate network error, or completing the login if the new connection is established quickly.

test cases.title: Orientation Change During Input
preconditions: User is on any authentication screen (Login, Register, Forgot Password).
steps: Enter partial text into an input field (e.g., email or password).
Change device orientation from portrait to landscape (or vice-versa).
test cases.expectedResult: The layout should adjust correctly to the new orientation, and the entered text in the input field should be preserved.
